Quick Summary: Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) / Intelligence Bureau (IB) has released IB MTS Tier-I Result 2026 on 13 April 2026 at official portal mha.gov.in. Candidates can download their merit list / shortlisted candidates list using Roll Number. Check qualifying status, cut-off marks, and next selection process below.

Are you waiting for IB MTS Tier-I Result 2026? Great news! The Intelligence Bureau under Ministry of Home Affairs has officially published the Tier-I result for Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS) posts today (13 April 2026). Thousands of candidates who appeared in the examination held on 27 January 2026 across various centres can now verify their qualifying status online.

This article provides complete information about IB MTS Tier-I Result 2026 including direct download links, merit list, expected cutoff marks, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

IB MTS 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Intelligence Bureau (IB), Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA)
Post Name Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS)
Result Type Tier-I Written Examination Result
Result Declaration Date 13 April 2026
Exam Conducted On 27 January 2026
Selection Stages Tier-I (Written) → Tier-II (Written/Skill Test) → Document Verification & Medical Exam
Official Website https://www.mha.gov.in

Check Your Result Now

Download IB MTS Tier-I Result 2026

Direct official link - Check merit list PDF (roll number wise)

How to Check IB MTS Tier-I Result 2026?

Candidates can download the IB MTS Tier-I Result 2026 merit list by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: https://www.mha.gov.in
  2. Go to the “Recruitment / Results” or “What’s New” section
  3. Click on the link “Result of MTS Tier-I Exam 2026” (or similar)
  4. Download the PDF file
  5. Open the PDF and search your Roll Number using Ctrl + F
  6. If your roll number appears in the list, you are shortlisted for Tier-II
  7. Download and take printout for future reference

IB MTS Tier-I Merit List 2026 - What’s Inside?

The IB MTS Tier-I merit list 2026 is a PDF document containing details of all shortlisted candidates for the next stage. IB prepares category-wise lists.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Numbers of shortlisted candidates
  • Category (Gen/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Qualifying Status
  • Cut-off marks (category-wise)

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Overall Merit List: All qualified candidates
  2. Category-Wise Merit List: Separate for OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.
  3. Waiting List: If applicable for future vacancies

IB MTS Tier-I Scorecard / Result 2026 - Information Breakdown

The result PDF / scorecard will display the following information:

Candidate Details Roll Number, Category
Exam Date 27 January 2026
Qualifying Status Qualified / Not Qualified for Tier-II
Cut-off Marks Category-wise minimum qualifying marks

Important Instructions for Qualified Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save the result PDF immediately
  • ✓ Take multiple printouts for future reference
  • ✓ Keep checking official website for Tier-II admit card / schedule
  • ✓ Start preparing for Tier-II examination / skill test
  • ✓ Keep all original documents ready for verification
  • ✓ Verify your details and report any discrepancy to IB immediately
  • ✓ Register / update mobile number for SMS alerts

As per the latest updates, the final marks and scorecard were made available on February 17, 2026, at the official website ssc.gov.in. This follows the declaration of the SSC GD final result on January 15, 2026.

Candidates who appeared in the PST/PET and DV/DME/RME stages can now download their SSC GD final scorecard 2025 using their registration number and password. The scorecard displays normalized CBT marks and overall qualifying status after all recruitment stages. The download window is limited - typically a short period - so act quickly before the link deactivates.

Key Highlights:

  • Release Date: February 17, 2026
  • Last Date to Download: March 1, 2026 (till 6:00 PM)
  • Access: Online only via ssc.gov.in login
  • Purpose: View final marks, compare with cut-offs, and save for records/joining

PET/PST and Medical Stages Conducted post-CBT shortlisting
Final Result Declaration January 15, 2026

Direct Link to Check SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Candidates can access the SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 through the direct link on the SSC portal under the “Result/Marks” section after logging in. The portal may redirect from legacy links, but use ssc.gov.in primarily.

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 – Direct Link (Active)
(Login required at ssc.gov.in for marks download)

Step-by-Step Guide to Download SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  1. Visit the official Staff Selection Commission website: ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” button on the homepage.
  3. Enter your registration number/username, password, and captcha (if prompted).
  4. After logging in, navigate to the “Result/Marks” tab on your dashboard.
  5. Select “Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination 2025” from the list.
  6. Click on the “SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025” or “Marks” link to view your details.
  7. Your final scorecard will display on screen - review marks, cut-off comparison, and status.
  8. Download the PDF and print it for future reference (e.g., document verification). Save it promptly, as access ends after the deadline.
  9. (Use the direct link mentioned above to reach the login faster if available.)

Details Mentioned on SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Your scorecard typically includes:

  • Candidate’s name, roll number, registration number
  • Category/sub-category (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.)
  • Exam name and applied post
  • Section-wise marks (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
  • Raw and normalized marks
  • Total marks in CBT (considered for final merit after qualifying PET/PST and medical)
  • Qualifying status (selected/not selected) and any remarks
  • Note on limited availability window

SSC GD Final Cut Off and Merit List 2025

The SSC GD final cut off 2025 was released with the final result on January 15, 2026, and is force-wise, state-wise, category-wise, and gender-wise. It appears in the official result PDF write-up.

Your scorecard marks should be compared to the relevant cut-off for your category/force/state to assess selection. The merit list PDF lists roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates (around 50,047 selected against vacancies), while the scorecard provides individual marks for those who reached physical/medical stages.

(Refer to the official SSC result PDFs for exact cut-off tables - examples range from ~104 to 152+ depending on category/force.)

What to Do After Downloading SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Selected candidates: Keep multiple printouts/PDFs of the scorecard and final result for document verification, joining, and force allocation.

Verify details (name, roll number, category, etc.) - report serious discrepancies to SSC regional offices promptly.

Non-selected candidates: Use marks for performance analysis and preparation for future SSC exams.

Download and save copies early - the window closes after the last date.

SSC GD Selection Process 2025 (Short Overview)

The process included:

  • Computer-Based Test (CBT): Four sections totaling 160 marks.
  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Qualifying.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME)/Review Medical Examination (RME): Qualifying.
  • Final merit based on normalized CBT marks (for qualified candidates), followed by force/state allocation per vacancies and preferences.

The final scorecard reflects CBT performance post-qualifying stages, available briefly post-result.

  • SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Download: ssc.gov.in (login to Result/Marks)
  • SSC GD Final Result 2025 Merit List PDF: Available on ssc.gov.in under Results
  • SSC GD Final Cut Off 2025 PDF: In final result write-up on ssc.gov.in
  • SSC Official Website: ssc.gov.in

Documents Required on RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies - same as uploaded in application)
  • Simple pen (blue/black ballpoint)

Optional but Recommended:

  • Pencil and eraser (for rough work)
  • Water bottle (transparent, without label)
  • Hand sanitizer (small bottle)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and smartphones
  • Smartwatches and digital watches
  • Calculators and electronic devices
  • Books, notes, and study materials
  • Bags and pouches
  • Bluetooth devices and earphones

Important Instructions for RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card 2-3 days before exam date
  • Check exam center location on Google Maps
  • Plan your route and transportation
  • Arrange all required documents in advance
  • Keep backup copies of admit card
  • Read exam instructions carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes 30 minutes before exam starts (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow dress code if specified in admit card
  • Carry water and light snacks for outside exam hall
  • Keep calm and focused

During Exam:

  • Follow all invigilator instructions carefully
  • Fill OMR sheet with black/blue ballpoint pen only
  • Manage your time wisely for all sections
  • Don't panic if questions seem difficult
  • Review answers if time permits
  • Don't carry prohibited items inside exam hall

What If RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again with a fresh session
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Safari)
  3. Solution 3: Check your application status on the official website to verify if your application was accepted
  4. Solution 4: Contact RSSB helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don't wait until exam day. Contact RSSB helpdesk at least 2 days before the exam.

SSC GD Constable Exam Pattern 2026

Understanding the exam pattern is crucial for effective preparation. Here's what candidates need to know about the SSC GD Constable examination:

  • Exam Mode: Online Computer Based Test
  • Total Marks: 160
  • Number of Questions: 80
  • Duration: 60 Minutes
  • Negative Marking: 0.50 marks for each wrong answer
  • Subjects/Sections: General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi

Preparation Tips for SSC GD Constable Exam

With the exam date announced, candidates should focus on smart preparation strategies:

1. Create a Study Schedule: Divide your time wisely among all subjects based on your strengths and weaknesses.

2. Focus on Previous Year Papers: Solve at least 5-10 previous year question papers to understand the pattern and difficulty level.

3. Take Mock Tests: Regular mock tests will help you manage time and identify areas needing improvement.

4. Revise Regularly: Make short notes and revise them daily, especially for GK and current affairs.

5. Stay Updated: Keep yourself updated with current affairs, especially related to CAPFs recruitment.

The SSC will release the admit card for GD Constable examination after the week preference window closes. Candidates can download their hall tickets from the official website using their registration number and date of birth.

  • Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhar Card/PAN Card/Driving License/Voter ID)
  • Recent passport-size photographs
  • Required stationery items (as per instructions)

What After the SSC GD Constable Exam?

After successfully appearing in the SSC GD Constable examination, candidates should:

1. Result Declaration: The result is typically declared within 30-45 days after the examination. Results will be available on the official website.

2. Answer Key: Preliminary answer keys may be released within 2-3 days after the exam. Candidates can raise objections if any discrepancy is found.

3. Next Stage: Qualified candidates will be called for Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Physical Standard Test (PST), Medical Examination & Document Verification as per the selection process.

Brief Information: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has announced a notification for the recruitment of Selection Post Phase 14 Vacancy. Eligible Candidates who are interested in the vacancy details & completed all eligibility criteria can read the notification & Apply.

SSC Recruitment 2026 Notification Overview

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has officially released a recruitment notification for Selection Post Phase 14. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

Selection Process

The competitive examination will be conducted through Computer Based Examination (CBE) of Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions.

  • Separate CBE for Matriculation, 10+2 and Graduation & above levels.
  • Scheme: 100 questions (25 each from General Intelligence, General Awareness, Quantitative Aptitude, English Language).
  • Duration: 60 Minutes (80 Minutes for scribes).
  • Negative Marking: 0.50 marks for each wrong answer.
  • Skill Tests (where prescribed) will be of qualifying nature.
  • Document Verification by User Departments after CBE.

How to Apply

  • Candidates must apply online only through the SSC website: https://ssc.gov.in or mySSC mobile app.
  • Online application window: 13-04-2026 to 04-05-2026 (Up to 2300 Hrs.).
  • Fee payment last date: 05-05-2026 (Up to 2300 Hrs.).
  • Application Form Correction window: 11-05-2026 to 13-05-2026.
  • Candidates can apply for more than one post separately.
  • Upload scanned Photograph and Signature in prescribed format.
  • No documents/certificates are required to be uploaded at the time of online application.
  • All details must be filled accurately as per original documents.
  • After successful submission and fee payment (where applicable), candidates will receive provisional admission to the examination.
  • Final selection is subject to Document Verification by User Departments.

General Instructions

  • Before filling the online application, candidates must read the full advertisement carefully.
  • All information provided in the online application must match the original certificates. Any mismatch detected during Document Verification will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • Provisional admission to the examination does not guarantee eligibility; final selection is subject to verification of original documents by User Departments.
  • All updates regarding examination, result, Document Verification, etc. will be available on the SSC website and User Department websites. Candidates must visit the websites regularly.
  • Application fee is non-refundable under any circumstances.
  • No request for change in category, sub-category, or any details will be accepted after submission.
  • Age, educational qualification, and other eligibility criteria will be considered as on the crucial dates mentioned in the Notice.
  • Reservations and age relaxations will be given as per prevailing Government rules (detailed in the advertisement).
  • PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and other reserved category candidates must produce valid certificates at the time of Document Verification.
  • In case of any dispute, the decision of the Staff Selection Commission shall be final and binding.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Candidates are advised to visit the official SSC website regularly for all updates.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Territorial Army Directorate Indian Army Territorial Army Commission Examination 2026 Notification has been released on the official website www.indianarmy.nic.in. A total of 12 vacancies (11 Male + 1 Female) have been announced for the post of Territorial Army Officer (Lieutenant rank) under the Indian Army.

Candidates aged 18 to 42 years who have completed Graduation from a recognised university and are gainfully employed are eligible to apply for these prestigious defence vacancies. The online application process for Territorial Army Commission 2026 will start on 20 April 2026 (10:00 hrs) and will remain open until 19 May 2026 (23:55 hrs).

The selection process includes a Computer Based Test (CBT) scheduled on 12 July 2026, followed by the SSB Interview. Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links provided below in this article.

Territorial Army 2026 - Important Dates

  • Application Window Opens: 20 April 2026 (10:00 hrs)    
  • Last Date to Apply Online: 19 May 2026 (23:55 hrs)  
  • Date of CBT (Written Exam): 12 July 2026
  • Admit Card Availability: 10-15 days before CBT
  • Objection Management Link Live: 72 hrs after exam (for 3 days)
  • SSB Interview: Post CBT (dates notified later)

Territorial Army 2026 - Overview

Particulars Details
Exam Name Territorial Army Commission Examination 2026
Post Territorial Army Officer (commissioned in the rank of Lieutenant)
Total Vacancies 12 (Male - 11 | Female - 01)
Nationality Indian citizens only (Male and Female)
Age Limit 18 to 42 years as on 19 May 2026
Educational Qualification Graduate from any recognised university
Application Mode Online only - www.indianarmy.nic.in
Application Fee Rs. 500/- (online payment only)
Exam Type Computer Based Test (CBT)
Exam Date 12 July 2026
Exam Centres Pan India - list available at www.indianarmy.nic.in
Official Website www.indianarmy.nic.in

Territorial Army Commission 2026 - Vacancy Details

Gender Vacanies 
Male 11
Female 01
Total 13

Male and female candidates will be placed in separate orders of merit based on total marks secured in the CBT and SSB tests. Vacancies are subject to change as per organisational requirement. Final selection will be made in order of merit up to the number of vacancies available.

Territorial Army Commission 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Nationality

Only citizens of India (male and female) are eligible to apply.

Age Limit

Candidates must have attained a minimum age of 18 years and must not be more than 42 years as on 19 May 2026 (last date of filing application).

Educational Qualification

Candidates must be a Graduate from any recognised university.

Physical Standards

Candidates must be physically and medically fit in all respects. Refer to the "Instructions to Candidates applying for Commission in The Territorial Army (Civilian Candidates)" uploaded on the Indian Army website for detailed guidelines.

Employment Condition

Candidates must be gainfully employed at the time of application. Serving members of the Regular Army, Navy, Air Force, Police, GREF, Para-Military and like forces are not eligible.

Territorial Army Commission 2026 - Application Fee

Application Fee: Rs. 500/- (Rupees Five Hundred Only)
Payment must be made only through the online modes prescribed on the official website. Payment by any other mode is not valid. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances, nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination.

Territorial Army Commission 2026 - Exam Pattern & Syllabus

The examination is a Computer Based Test (CBT) conducted in a single session of 2 hours. The question paper consists of 100 MCQ carrying 100 marks, divided into three parts.

Paper Subject No. of Questions Marks Time
Part 1 Reasoning 40 40 02 Hours
            (Single Session)
Part 2 General Knowledge 30 30
Part 3 English 30 30
Total 100 100  

ⓘ Negative Marking: For each wrong answer, 1/3 (0.33%) of the marks assigned to that question will be deducted as penalty. Questions marked for review will be treated as unanswered.

Detailed Syllabus

  • Part 1 - Reasoning: Tests the ability to complete sequences and make logical conclusions based on simple patterns of numbers, statements, figures, and letters — as expected of a rational thinking person without any special study.
  • Part 2 - General Knowledge: Covers General Knowledge and Current Affairs (equal weightage). Includes questions on current affairs, everyday scientific observation, History of India, and Geography — answerable without special study.    
  • Part 3 - English: Tests understanding of English and workmanlike use of words.

The standard of papers will approximately be that expected of a graduate of an Indian university. The Territorial Army Directorate has the discretion to fix qualifying marks in any or all subjects.

Territorial Army Commission 2026 - Selection Process

  • Stage 1 - CBT: Candidates whose application forms are found correct and who qualify the CBT will be detailed for Intelligence and Personality Test at the Service Selection Board (SSB).    
  • Stage 2 - SSB (Two-Stage): A two-stage selection procedure based on Psychological Aptitude Test and Intelligence Test is followed at SSBs. All candidates will be put to Stage 1 (Screening) on the first day of reporting. Only those qualifying Stage 1 will be admitted to the Stage 2/remaining tests. Those who fail Stage 1 will be returned.   
  • Final Merit: Male and female candidates will be placed in separate orders of merit based on total marks in CBT and SSB tests combined.

Territorial Army Commission 2026 - Embodiment, Training & Service Conditions

Embodiment for Training

  • Up to 6 months Pre-Commission Training as provided in TA Rule 20A (amended vide SRO 65 dated 29 Apr 2023). Candidates will be commissioned in TA only on successful completion of this training, after which 21 days Post-Commission Leave will be granted.
  • 30 days training under Rule-19 after completion of post-commission leave.
  • Two months annual training camp every year (if not embodied for more than six months in a training year — April to March).

Terms and Conditions of Service

  • Territorial Army is a part-time concept with mandatory two months training per year. It does not guarantee a full-time career.
  • Serving in TA does not guarantee pension — subject to embodied service as per organisational requirement.
  • Commission is granted in the rank of Lieutenant.
  • Pay and Allowances will be the same as Regular Army Officers when embodied for training and military service.
  • Promotions up to Lt Colonel by time scale (subject to laid-down criteria). Promotion to Colonel and Brigadier by selection.
  • Officers commissioned in TA may be called out for military service for longer duration depending on requirements.

Pay Scales (7th CPC)

Rank Level Pay Matrix Military Service Pay
Lieutenant Level 10 Rs. 56,100 - 1,77,500 Rs. 15,500/-
Captain Level 10A Rs. 61,300 - 1,93,900 Rs. 15,500/-
Major Level 11 Rs. 69,400 - 2,07,200 Rs. 15,500/-
Lt Colonel Level 12A Rs. 1,21,200 - 2,12,400 Rs. 15,500/-
Colonel Level 13 Rs. 1,30,600 - 2,15,900 Rs. 15,500/-
Brigadier Level 13A Rs. 1,39,600 - 2,17,600 Rs. 15,500/-

Documents Required at SSB Interview

Candidates who qualify the CBT must bring the following documents in original with self-attested copies at the time of SSB Interview:

  •  Application Form IAF (TA)-9 (Revised) Part-2 — downloaded from www.indianarmy.nic.in and filled in own handwriting    
  • All educational qualification certificates (Matric onwards).
  • Copy of identity proof with photograph (Voter ID / PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Aadhaar etc.).   
  • Proof of residential address.
  • Certificate for proof of age (Matric/Senior Secondary mark sheet and certificate for DOB verification).
  • Candidates with name variation in documents: copy of Gazette Notification of India/State showing correct name, OR an affidavit supported by newspaper cuttings.
  • Latest income proof from appropriate authority (Income Tax Revenue Department / Magistrate / Employer).
  • Copy of latest Income Tax Return (ITR) filed.
  • Admit Card.
  • Self-employed candidates: Affidavit on Non-Judicial stamp paper (minimum value, duly attested by Notary) stating nature of employment and annual income, along with photocopy of PAN Card and self-certified character certificate.
  • Govt./Semi-Govt./Private Sector employed candidates: Service certificate authenticated by Head of Office, along with salary certificate and No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the department as per the format prescribed in the notification.

How to Apply for Territorial Army Commission 2026

Applications must be submitted online only at www.indianarmy.nic.in. No other mode of application is allowed.

Apply Online - indianarmy.nic.in

1. Visit the official website: Go to www.indianarmy.nic.in and read all instructions for filling the online Application Form IAF (TA)-9 (Revised) Part-1 before proceeding.

2. Fill Application Form: Complete the online application form from 20 April 2026 (10:00 hrs) to 19 May 2026 (23:55 hrs). Provide a valid and active email ID and mobile number — all communications will be sent electronically. Select your examination centre carefully, as no change of centre will be granted.

3. Pay Application Fee: Pay Rs. 500/- through the prescribed online payment modes available on the website. Applications submitted without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected.

4. Submit and Save: Submit the application well before the last date. Save a printout/copy of the filled and submitted application form for future reference.

5. Download Admit Card: The admit card will be made available online 10 to 15 days before the CBT (12 July 2026). An email and SMS alert will also be sent. The admit card will include photograph, scanned signature, date of birth, exam date/time, and examination centre details.

Selection Process

The recruitment process will consist of Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Physical Standards Test (PST), Computer Based Test (CBT), Trade/Skill Test, Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (DME/RME).

  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Qualifying in nature.
    • For Constable (Driver, Motor Mechanic Vehicle, Gardner, Painter, Carpenter, Brass Band, Pipe Band, Cobbler, Tailor & Bugler): Male – 5 Kms race in 24 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 8.30 minutes.
    • For Constable (Cook/Water Carrier, Barber & Hair Dresser, Washer man & Washer women, Safai Karmachari): Male – 1.6 Kms in 10 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 12 minutes.
    • For Pioneer Wing (Mason / Electrician): Male – 1.6 Kms in 09 minutes.
    • Pregnant women candidates will be declared temporarily unfit.
  • Physical Standards Test (PST): Height, Chest (for males only) & Weight (proportionate to height & age).
    • Height: Male – 170 Cms (minimum); Female – 157 Cms (minimum). Relaxations applicable for certain categories (ST, NE States, Gorkhas, etc.).
    • Chest (Male only): Un-expanded – 80 Cms, Minimum expansion – 05 Cms. Relaxations applicable.
    • Candidates failing PST will be eliminated.
  • Computer Based Test (CBT): 100 marks (100 MCQs), duration 02 hours.
    • General Intelligence & Reasoning – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • General Knowledge & General Awareness – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • Elementary Mathematics – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • English/Hindi – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • Negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.
    • Qualifying marks: UR – 30%, EWS/OBC – 25%, SC/ST & others – 20%.
    • Marks will be normalized. Merit list prepared on CBT marks.
  • Trade/Skill Test: Qualifying in nature (50 marks, minimum 20 marks required). Practical assessment of the applied trade/post.
  • Document Verification (DV): Original documents (Matric certificate, Domicile/PRC, Trade certificates, Caste/EWS/ESM certificates, etc.) will be verified.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME) / Review Medical Examination (RME): As per Uniform Guidelines issued by MHA. Temporary unfitness allowed only for pregnancy. Final selection subject to medical fitness.
  • Final merit list prepared on the basis of marks obtained in CBT (subject to qualifying all stages). Separate merit lists for male/female and each State/UT (All India basis for Pioneer posts).

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through CRPF website. No other mode will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website www.rect.crpf.gov.in.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment for the post of Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) 2026”.
  3. Register by providing basic details and generate Provisional Registration Number & Password.
  4. Fill the online application form carefully (personal details, educational qualification, trade/post applied, State/UT of domicile, etc.).
  5. Upload scanned copies of recent colour passport size photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per specifications.
  6. Pay the application fee online (if applicable) through BHIM, UPI, Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card or CSC Wallet.
  7. Submit the application and take printout of the application form & e-receipt for future reference.
  8. Candidates should bring two coloured printouts of the Admit Card at every stage of examination.

General Instructions

  • Recruitment is domicile-based for Technical & Tradesmen posts and All India basis for Pioneer posts. Candidates must submit valid Domicile/Permanent Residence Certificate of the applied State/UT at DV/DME.
  • Candidates must be citizens of India.
  • Disqualification: Candidates who have entered into or contracted marriage with a person having a spouse living or who, having a spouse living, have entered into or contracted marriage with any person (subject to personal law exemptions).
  • Age & educational qualification will be reckoned as on 01-01-2026.
  • Only Indian Nationals need to apply. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in PET/PST/CBT/Trade Test/DV/DME.
  • Mere submission of application or issue of Admit Card does not confer any right to selection.
  • The CRPF reserves the right to cancel/restrict/enlarge/modify the recruitment process without assigning any reason.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts having jurisdiction over the Examination Centres.
  • All correspondence will be made only through the registered email ID/mobile number provided by the candidate.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.rect.crpf.gov.in regularly for updates.
  • For any queries, candidates may contact CRPF Helpline No. 011-26160255.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Indian Navy (Indian Navy) Agniveer (MR/Musician) Notification 2026 has been released on 11 April 2026 on the official website https://www.joinindiannavy.gov.in/. The vacancies will be decided based on service requirement through the Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026.

Candidates born between 01 Jul 2004 to 31 Dec 2008 who have passed Matriculation (10th) with minimum 50% marks along with musical proficiency are eligible to apply for these Agniveer (Musician) vacancies. The online application process for Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 has started on 11 April 2026 and will remain open until 26 April 2026.

Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links provided below in this article.

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Online applications are invited for Agniveer (MR/Musician) - 02/2026 (Jul 26) batch on the official website joinindiannavy.gov.in. Candidates should thoroughly read the official advertisement to check eligibility criteria, age limit, musical standards, selection process and detailed instructions.

Document Download Link
Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) 02/2026 Batch Official Notification Download Notification PDF

Training Commencement July 2026 at INS Chilka, Odisha

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification: Candidate must have passed Matriculation Examination with minimum 50% marks in aggregate from the Boards of School Education recognised by Ministry of Education, Govt. of India.

Age Limit: Born between 01 Jul 2004 – 31 Dec 2008 (both dates inclusive).

Marital Status: Only unmarried male and unmarried female candidates are eligible. Candidates will have to give a certificate of being 'unmarried' at the time of enrolment. Agniveers shall not be permitted to marry during their entire tenure of four years. Marriage during tenure will result in dismissal.

Musical Ability: The candidate should have proficiency and aptitude for music with accuracy in tempo, pitch and singing one complete song. He/She should also have actual practical skill on any instrument of Indian or foreign origin with proficiency in tuning of instrument, matching unknown notes with instrument, prepared piece, sight reading of Indian Classical/Tablature/Staff Notation, performance of Scale/Raaga/Taala and basic theory of Indian or International music.

Type of Instruments on which Proficiency Required: Candidates would be required to perform with expertise on one or more instruments such as Keyboard/String/Wind instruments or any other instrument of Indian or foreign origin. Candidates performing on indefinite pitch instruments such as Jazz Drum set, Bass Drum, Snare Drums, Side Drums, Cymbals, Non Chromatic instruments such as bugle & monotone instruments used to produce only one note or drone will not be eligible.

Musical Experience Certificate:

  • Candidates performing Hindustani or Carnatic Classical music on wind instruments will require a musical experience certificate from any recognized musical institute or organisation of repute.
  • A certificate of minimum initial grade from international boards such as Trinity College of Music, London or Royal School of Music London etc., will be mandatory for candidates performing western notation or western music on wind instruments.
  • Other candidates will be required to submit certificates of participation/awards presented at various events.

Physical Standards:

  • Minimum Height: 157 cm (Male & Female)
  • Visual Standards: 6/12, 6/12 (Uncorrected) | 6/6, 6/6 (Corrected) | CP Pass

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Pay, Allowances & Benefits

Agniveers will be paid a package of ₹30,000 per month with a fixed yearly increment. In addition, risk and hardship, dress and travel allowances will be paid.

Seva Nidhi Package (One-time on completion of 4 years):

Year Customised Package (Monthly) In Hand (70%) Contribution to Agniveer Corpus Fund (30%) Contribution to Corpus Fund by GoI
1st Year 30,000 21,000 9,000 9,000
2nd Year 33,000 23,100 9,900 9,900
3rd Year 36,500 25,550 10,950 10,950
4th Year 40,000 28,000 12,000 12,000
Total - - 5.02 Lakh 5.02 Lakh
  • Life Insurance Cover: ₹48 Lakh (non-contributory)
  • Death Compensation: ₹44 Lakh (one-time ex-gratia) + Insurance
  • Disability Compensation: ₹44/25/15 Lakh (based on % disability)

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

Selection of recruits is based on the order of merit based on their performance in Music Ability Test.

  1. Shortlisting: Based on marks obtained in Matriculation examination (All India merit). Only candidates holding valid music certificates will be considered.
  2. Recruitment Process: Physical Fitness Test (PFT) → Written Examination → Music Ability Test → Recruitment Medical Examination.
  3. Physical Fitness Test (PFT) – Qualifying:
Gender 1.6 Km Run Squats (Uthak Baithak) Push-ups Bent Sit-ups Knee Sit-ups
Male 06 min 30 sec 20 15 15 15
Female 08 min 15 10 10 10
  1. Written Examination: 50 MCQs (Science & Mathematics + General Awareness), 30 minutes, bilingual, minimum 25% marks required.
  2. Music Ability Test: Qualifying for final selection.
  3. Recruitment Medical Examination & Pre-Enrolment Medicals at INS Chilka.

Final Merit List: Based on performance in Music Ability Test (All India basis).

How to Apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must apply online through the official Indian Navy portal before the last date.

Step 1 – Visit www.joinindiannavy.gov.in and register.
Step 2 – Fill the online application form with correct details and upload scanned copies of all required documents (including music certificates, Matriculation marksheet, photograph with slate).
Step 3 – Verify and submit the application.
Step 4 – Download and print the application for future reference.

Important Note: Recent passport size colour photograph (taken not before Mar 2026) with name and date written on slate is mandatory. Applications through speed post/registered post will not be accepted.

Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) Executive Trainee (ET) Recruitment 2026 Notification has been released on 10 April 2026 on the official website www.npcilcareers.co.in. A total of 330 vacancies have been announced through the NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification: B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc. (Engineering) or 5-year Integrated M.Tech with a minimum of 60% aggregate marks in one of the 6 advertised disciplines from a university/institution recognised by AICTE/UGC. Only full-time, on-campus regular degrees are eligible — correspondence, distance education, part-time, and research-based degrees are not accepted.

GATE Score: Candidates must hold a valid GATE 2024, GATE 2025, or GATE 2026 score in the same discipline as their qualifying degree. GATE 2023 and earlier scores are not valid. Equal weightage is given to all three years' GATE scores.

Final Year Applicants: Candidates awaiting results or appearing in their final semester/year exam during 2026 may apply, but must submit their final result (minimum 60%) by 30 November 2026 if selected.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Stipend & Pay Scale

During Training: Monthly Stipend — Rs. 74,000/- | One-time Book Allowance — Rs. 30,000/- | Mandatory lodging & boarding provided by NPCIL.

After Appointment as Scientific Officer/C (Group A): Pay Level 10 — Rs. 56,100/- in Pay Matrix + 58% DA (as on 01.07.2025) + Transport Allowance, HRA, Site Location Allowance, Professional Update Allowance, LTA, Medical (CHSS), Children Education Assistance, Performance Linked Incentive, EPF, Gratuity, Group Insurance, and NPCIL Defined Contribution Pension Scheme 2025.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Application Fee

Category Fee
General / EWS / OBC (Male candidates only) Rs. 500/- (non-refundable)
Female candidates Nil (exempted)
SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-servicemen / DODPKIA / NPCIL employees Nil (exempted)

Fee is payable online only via debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI through the integrated payment gateway. No other mode accepted.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Selection Process

Candidates are shortlisted for Personal Interview based on GATE 2024/2025/2026 scores in a 1:12 ratio (discipline and category wise). GATE score carries no weightage in the final selection — final selection is purely based on Personal Interview performance and medical fitness.

Stage Details
Shortlisting Based on GATE 2024/2025/2026 score (1:12 ratio, merit list discipline & category wise)
Personal Interview Tentatively 8–19 June 2026 at Mumbai, NAPS (UP), MAPS (Tamil Nadu), KGS (Karnataka)
Qualifying marks (Interview) UR — 70% | EWS/SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD — 60%
Tie-breaking Higher GATE score → if still tied, senior in age ranks higher
Medical fitness Mandatory — by NPCIL's authorised medical officer
Bond Rs. 9,18,000/- indemnity bond + service bond for minimum 3 years after training

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Training Details

Selected candidates undergo a one-year orientation training at one of NPCIL's Nuclear Training Centres (NTC). Candidates in Mechanical, Chemical, Electrical, Electronics, and Instrumentation disciplines must choose a Reactor Stream — either Pressurised Heavy Water Reactor (PHWR) or Light Water Reactor (LWR). Civil discipline candidates need not indicate a reactor stream preference. After successful training completion, candidates are appointed as Scientific Officer/C in Group A on a one-year probation.

How to Apply for NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026

1. Register: Visit www.npcilcareers.co.in and click Apply → Online Registration. Enter your GATE registration number (from GATE 2024/2025/2026 admit card), discipline, personal details, and category. An activation link will be sent to your registered email.

2. Activate your account: Click the activation link in your email to activate your login.

3. Fill application form: Log in and complete all stages — educational qualifications, post-qualification work experience (if any), personal details, interview city preference, reactor stream choice (if applicable), and upload scanned photo (JPEG, max 50 KB) and signature (JPEG, max 20 KB).

4. Submit application: Review the draft carefully and click "Submit Application." No changes can be made after submission.

5. Pay application fee (if applicable): General/EWS/OBC male candidates pay Rs. 500/- online via debit/credit card, net banking, or UPI. Fee payment window: 10 April – 30 April 2026. Note your application number on successful payment.

Other Terms and Conditions

How to Apply for CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Recruitment 2026

Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online only through the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website. Follow the steps given below to submit your application form.

Visit Official Website

  • Go to the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website: csc.gov.in.

  • On the homepage, navigate to the Recruitment/Careers/Latest Opening section.

Open Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Application Link

  • Find and click on the notification/link titled “CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Recruitment 2026” or similar.

  • Click on the Apply Online button to proceed to the application page.

Registration (New Users)

  • Click on New Registration (if applying for the first time).

  • Enter basic details such as Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, Date of Birth, and create a password.

  • Submit the details to generate your Registration ID and Password (note these for future login).

Login and Fill Application Form

  • Login using your Registration ID and Password.

  • Fill in the online application form carefully with personal details, contact details, educational qualification, Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator certificate details, and preferred state/district.

  • Ensure that all details match your certificates and Aadhaar data.

Upload Documents

  • Upload scanned copies of the following documents in the prescribed format and size (as mentioned in the notification):

    • Recent passport size photograph

    • Signature

    • 10th/12th/ITI/Polytechnic Diploma certificates and mark sheets

    • Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate issued by UIDAI‑authorized Testing & Certifying Agency

    • Any other documents required as per notification

  • Preview the uploaded documents and confirm that they are clear and readable.

Application Fee Payment (If Applicable)

  • Check the Application Fee section in the official notification.

  • If any fee is prescribed, pay it online through available modes such as Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI.

  • After successful payment, download/print the fee receipt.

Final Submission

  • Carefully review all the details filled in the application form in the Preview section.

  • If required, make corrections before final submission.

  • Click on Final Submit to complete the application process.

Print Application Form

  • After successful submission, download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.

  • Keep the Registration ID, Password, and printout safe for further recruitment stages.

Brief Information: The Central Bank of India has announced a notification for the recruitment of Specialist Officers Vacancy. Eligible Candidates who are interested in the vacancy details & completed all eligibility criteria can read the notification & Apply.

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Notification Overview

The Central Bank of India has officially released a recruitment notification for Specialist Officers. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

Central Bank of India

Specialist Officers Vacancy 2026

WWW.OnlyJobs

Application Fee

  • SC/ST/PwBD/Women: ₹175/- (Intimation Charges only)
  • All Other Candidates: ₹850/- (Application Fee + Intimation Charges)

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

  • Starting Date for Apply Online: 09-04-2026
  • Last Date for Apply Online: 30-04-2026
  • Tentative Date of Online Examination: May 2026
  • Tentative Date of Interview: June 2026

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

  • Age limit Scale IV: 20-45 Years
  • Age limit Scale III / II: 20-35 Years
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

Qualification

  • Analytics & Model Risk (Scale IV/III): Bachelor’s in Mathematics/Statistics or BE/B.Tech in Data Science/IT/Computer Science/Software Engineering + 5/3 years post-qualification experience in quantitative/risk analytics/model validation (Banks/NBFC/Investment Banks). Preferred: MBA/PGDM in Finance, FRM/CFA/CQF certifications.
  • Asset Liability Management (Scale III/II): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management in Banks/NBFCs).
  • Digital Risk Management (Scale III/II): Bachelor’s in Computer Science/IT/Cybersecurity + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management). Preferred: CISA/CISM/CRISC/CISSP.
  • Enterprise Risk (Scale III): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 5 years total experience (at least 3 years in Risk Management).
  • Market Risk (Scale III/II): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management).

Remuneration (Scale of Pay)

  • Scale IV (Chief Manager): ₹102300-2980/4-114220-3360/2-120940
  • Scale III (Senior Manager): ₹85920-2680/5-99320-2980/2-105280
  • Scale II (Manager): ₹64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960
  • (Perquisites/allowances as per Bank’s policy)

Selection Process

  • The selection process for Central Bank of India Specialist Officers in Risk Management streams consists of an online written examination followed by a personal interview.

  • The final merit will be prepared by giving 70% weightage to the online written test and 30% weightage to the interview, subject to document verification and medical fitness.

  • Only those candidates who qualify the online exam and meet the bank’s cutoff will be called for the interview in a prescribed ratio to the number of vacancies.

How to Apply

Visit official website

  • Go to the official Central Bank of India website: centralbank.bank.in.

  • Open the “Recruitment/ Careers” section and click on the link for “Recruitment Process for Selection of Officers in Specialist Category in Risk Management Streams.”

Open Apply Online link

  • On the recruitment page, click on “Click Here to Apply” / “Apply Online” which redirects to the online registration page (IBPS application link for CBI SO).

  • Read the detailed notification, “How to Apply” and FAQ on the registration page carefully before proceeding.

New registration

  • Click on “New Registration” and enter basic details (name, contact number, email ID, etc.) to generate a Registration Number and Password.

  • Note down the Registration Number and Password for future login and form completion.

Fill online application form

  • Log in using Registration Number and Password and select the relevant post/scale (Analytics & Model Risk, Asset Liability Management, Digital Risk Management, Enterprise Risk, Market Risk in Scale IV/III/II as applicable).

  • Fill personal details, category, domicile, address, qualification (B.Sc/B.Tech/BE/MBA/PGDM etc.) and post-qualification experience in Risk/IT/ALM/Digital Risk as per eligibility.

  • Ensure all experience details (years, organisation, role) match what is required for the chosen scale and stream.

Upload required documents

  • Upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration in the prescribed dimensions and file size, as per the instructions in the notification.

  • Upload supporting documents (if asked at application stage) like educational certificates, experience certificates and category certificates in the specified format.

Pay application fee

  • Pay the application fee online: ₹175/- (intimation charges) for SC/ST/PwBD/Women candidates and ₹850/- (application fee + intimation charges) for all other candidates, through debit card/credit card/net banking/UPI.

  • After successful payment, verify that the fee status shows as “paid” and proceed to final submit. Applications without successful fee payment (where applicable) will be treated as incomplete.

Final submission and print

  • Review all details carefully; edit if required before final submission, as no changes are allowed after final submit.

  • Click on “Final Submit” and generate your application form.

  • Download and take a printout of the e-receipt and submitted application form for future reference, online exam and interview/document verification stages.

General Instructions for Candidates

  • Apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted.

  • Ensure you fall within the required age limits for Scale IV (20–45 years) and Scale III/II (20–35 years) with applicable relaxations as per Government/Bank rules.

  • Enter details exactly as per your certificates; any mismatch or false information may lead to rejection at any stage of the recruitment process.

  • Keep the registered mobile number and email ID active; all communication regarding exam date, admit card, interview schedule, etc., will be sent on these only.

  • Candidates are advised to complete the application process well before 30-04-2026 to avoid last-minute technical issues.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About this page: Sarkari Job 2026 information on this page is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team from official government recruitment notifications. All vacancy data is verified against official websites before listing. OnlyJobs has been India's trusted government job portal since 2011, serving 10+ million users annually. For any recruitment, always refer to the official notification linked above.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering
  • Category-wise Jobs: General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, PwD

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 820+ active job listings.

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Apply Online wcd.nic.in

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – Apply online for the latest Anganwadi Worker, Anganwadi Helper and Supervisor vacancies through the official WCD/ICDS portals such as wcd.nic.in and state ICDS portals. Candidates can check state-wise and district-wise Anganwadi vacancy lists, eligibility, qualifications, age limit, salary, and online application dates, and download the Anganwadi Application Form 2026. Start your career in Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) and stay updated with the latest Anganwadi job notifications from all major states including Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Bihar and more.

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category
Forest Guard / Van Rakshak / Vana Palaka Level 3 ₹21,700 ₹28,000 – ₹35,000 Group C
Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B
Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹75,000 – ₹95,000 Group B

Additional PCCF (Addl. PCCF) Level 15 ₹1,82,200 ₹2,10,000 – ₹2,20,000 IFS Cadre
Principal Chief Conservator (PCCF / HoFF) Level 17 ₹2,25,000 ₹2,25,000 (fixed) Apex Scale

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download State-Wise PDF @indiapostgdsonline.gov.in

Quick Summary: Department of Posts (India Post) has released India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 on 10 April 2026 at official portal indiapostgdsonline.gov.in / indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement. Candidates can check their selection status using Registration Number in state-wise/circle-wise PDFs. Check qualifying details, document verification schedule, and next steps below.

Are you waiting for India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026? Great news! The Department of Posts has officially published the supplementary 2nd Merit List for Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS) recruitment today on 10 April 2026. Thousands of candidates who applied for BPM, ABPM, and Dak Sevak posts (total 28,635+ vacancies) can now verify their selection status online. Lists for Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal are withheld due to Model Code of Conduct.

This article provides complete information about India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 including direct state-wise PDF links, how to check selection status, merit list details, and step-by-step instructions.

India Post GDS 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Department of Posts, India Post
Post Name Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS) - BPM, ABPM, Dak Sevak
Result Type 2nd Supplementary Merit List
Result Declaration Date 10 April 2026
Application Period 31 January to 14 February 2026
1st Merit List Date 06 March 2026
Selection Stages Merit based on Class 10 Marks → Document Verification → Appointment
Official Website indiapostgdsonline.gov.in / indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement

Check Your 2nd Merit List Now

Direct Link to Download India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026

Direct official portal - State-wise / Circle-wise PDFs available

How to Check India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026?

Candidates can check their India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: https://indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement or https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in/
  2. Go to “Candidate’s Corner” or “Online Engagement” section
  3. Click on the **2nd Merit List / Supplementary List - II** link
  4. Select your Postal Circle / State from the list
  5. Download the PDF
  6. Press Ctrl + F and search your 7-digit **Registration Number**
  7. Check your name, marks, division, post, and document verification details

India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 contains details of newly shortlisted candidates for remaining vacancies. Separate supplementary lists are released circle-wise.

Merit List Contains:

  • Registration Number
  • Candidate Name
  • Division / Office Name
  • Post ID & Post Name (BPM / ABPM / Dak Sevak)
  • Class 10 Marks (calculated to 4 decimal places)
  • Category
  • Document Verification Date & Venue
  • Required Documents List

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Supplementary List - II (2nd Merit List): Released on 10 April 2026
  2. Circle-wise PDFs: Available for 19 circles (Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu & West Bengal withheld)
  3. Further Supplementary Lists: May be released if vacancies remain

Important Instructions for Selected Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save the merit list PDF immediately
  • ✓ Attend Document Verification on the given date at the Divisional Office with all originals + 2 sets of photocopies
  • ✓ Required documents: Class 10 Marksheet & Certificate, ID Proof, Category Certificate (if applicable), Domicile Certificate, Photographs, etc.
  • ✓ Selection is purely provisional; final appointment depends on successful DV and verification
  • ✓ Keep checking the official portal regularly for further updates or additional lists
  • ✓ No waiting list; vacancies are filled through subsequent merit lists
